checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is a highly visual annual report with integrated diagrams, charts, and maps that define its organizational flow. The HTML version collapses this complex layout into a simple single-column text-only document. While linearization is expected, the removal of core structural diagrams like Figure 2 (Coordination) and Figure 3 (Statutory Committees) significantly changes the visual organization and flow of the document, making it feel incomplete compared to the source's design intent.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF pages 7 and 8 show a diagram-centric layout explaining the Council's structure ('GICC Coordination and Collaboration' and 'GICC Statutory Committees').
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML version presents these sections as plain text without any corresponding structural diagrams or organizational flow markers.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'About the Council' and 'What is GIS?' (PDF pages 7-8)

checks.a_analysis: The tables present in the document (Table 1 and Table 2) are correctly rendered in the HTML. They maintain distinct headers, aligned columns, and readable rows. A sighted user can easily interpret the data as intended in the source.

A vast majority of the meaningful images and figures present in the source PDF are missing from the HTML output. Out of approximately 34 figures (photos, charts, maps, and diagrams), only the cover image and Figure 1 are present. Key informative visuals such as Figure 6 (GMA Report Card), Figure 11 (Broadband Dashboard), and Figure 23 (C-CAP Landcover) are completely absent.
checks.b_evidence_source: Figures 2 through 32, including diagrams (Fig 2, 3), infographics (Fig 6), and data dashboards (Fig 11, 26).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML document only contains the cover map image and Figure 1; all other meaningful images are missing, leaving only captions.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document from PDF page 7 to page 44

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output was reviewed for repeated content blocks, paragraphs, or tables. No instances of visual duplication were found; the text flow correctly reflects the sequential order of the source PDF.
